titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of BL Cotton Ch. xviii.45

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=626 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:23:30.860623+00 | translation moa=627 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:23:28.891853+00

div.p:
. D’. dei gratia Rex Scott’ Omnibus Baronibus suis . et hominibus . et Amicis 
francis . et Angl’  salutem . Sciatis me dedisse . et concessisse Roberto de
Brus Estrahanent . et totam terram a diuisa Dunegal de Stranit
usque ad Diuisam Rand’ Meschin’ . Et uolo et concedo . ut illam
terram . et suum castellum bene . et honorifice  cum omnibus consuetudinibus
suis teneat . et habeat . videlicet cum omnibus illis consuetudinibus quas Rand’ Meschin unquam habuit In Carduill’ . et In terra sua de Cumber
land’ illo die in quo unquam meliores . et liberiores habuit . T’ . Eusat’ fil’
Iohannis . et Hug’ de Morvill’ . et Alan’ de Perci et Will’ de Sumervill’ . et Bereng’
Inganio . et Rand’ . de Sules . et Walt’ de Morvill’ . et Herui fil’ Warini
et Edmund’ Camer’ . Apud Sconam .

David by the grace of God king of the Scots to all his barons, and men, and friends, French and English, greeting. Know me to have given and conceded Annandale [Estrahanent] to Robert de Brus, and the whole land from the boundary of Dunegal of Nithsdale [Stranit] as far as the boundary of Ranulf le Meschin. And I will and concede that he hold and have the land and its castle well and honourably with all its customs, namely, with all those customs which Ranulf le Meschin ever had in Carlisle and his land of Cumberland on that day in which he ever had them best and most freely. As witness: Eustace fitz John; Hugh de Morville; Alan de Percy; William de Somerville; Berengar Engaine; Ranulf de Soules; Walter de Morville; Hervey fitz Warin; Edmund the chamberlain. At Scone.
